overview: Runs an external command-line program on a raw document during pipeline processing. The step provides the input document path and expects the command to produce an output document at a specified path. Variable substitution is available for paths, source/target language information, and the project root directory.

command.help:
The command line to execute. Must use `${inputPath}` and `${outputPath}` variables to read the input document and write the output document.

Available variables (case-sensitive):

| Variable | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `${inputPath}` | Full path of the input document | |
| `${outputPath}` | Full path of the output document | |
| `${srcLangName}` | English name of source language | `"German"` for `de-ch` |
| `${trgLangName}` | English name of target language | `"Japanese"` for `ja-jp` |
| `${srcLang}` | Source language code | `"de"` for `de-ch` |
| `${trgLang}` | Target language code | `"ja"` for `ja-jp` |
| `${srcBCP47}` | Source BCP-47 tag | `"de-CH"` for `de-CH` |
| `${trgBCP47}` | Target BCP-47 tag | `"ja-JP"` for `ja-JP` |
| `${rootDir}` | Project/batch root directory | In Rainbow: the parameters folder |
command.values: A shell command string with `${variable}` placeholders. Variable names are **case-sensitive**.
notes: Variable names are case-sensitive — `${inputpath}` will **not** work.

timeout.help:
Maximum number of seconds the external command is allowed to run before being forcibly cancelled. Use `-1` to allow the command to run indefinitely.

Set an appropriate timeout to prevent the pipeline from hanging on commands that may stall or run longer than expected.
timeout.values: Integer seconds, or `-1` for no timeout.

limitations: None known per the Okapi wiki.
processingNotes: Takes a **raw document** as input and sends a **raw document** as output — the step operates on the file-level, not on extracted text units.
The step requires both source and target languages to be set in the pipeline for the language variables to resolve.

examples.title: Sort file contents (Windows)
examples.description: Uses the Windows `sort` command to sort lines in the input file and write the result to the output path.
